title: Google Cloud load balancers SSL policies should not offer weak cipher suites
description: SSL policies for Google Cloud load balancers determine which cipher suites are available for client connections. Allowing weak ciphers (e.g., RC4, DES, export-grade) exposes traffic to interception and decryption by attackers. Only strong, modern cipher suites should be enabled to ensure confidentiality and integrity.
message: SSL policy offers weak cipher suites. Restrict to strong, secure ciphers only.
impact: Weak ciphers can be exploited to decrypt sensitive data in transit, leading to data breaches and regulatory violations.
recommendation: Configure SSL policies to use only strong ciphers (e.g., TLS 1.2+ with AES-GCM). Regularly review and update policies to remove deprecated ciphers.

noncompliant.description: The 'COMPATIBLE' profile and TLS 1.0 allow weak ciphers, exposing traffic to risk.

compliant.description: The 'MODERN' profile and TLS 1.2 restrict ciphers to strong, secure options.

devsecops.security: Strong ciphers protect data in transit from eavesdropping and tampering.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD should enforce secure SSL policy profiles and minimum TLS versions.
devsecops.compliance: Strong encryption is required by PCI DSS, HIPAA, and other standards.

why_is_this_an_issue: Weak ciphers can be broken by attackers, exposing sensitive data and violating compliance requirements.
how_to_fix: Set SSL policy profile to 'MODERN' or equivalent, and require TLS 1.2 or higher. Remove any deprecated ciphers from allowed lists.

security_mappings.CERT: MSC61-CPP: Do not use deprecated or weak cryptographic algorithms
